refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions. Verify compliance with safety plan via incident/near-miss logs and safety audit checklist.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Tele-operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle via Teleoperation with sufficient latency, bandwidth, and feedback fidelity to sustain productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services shall continuously monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, load limits, stability) without degrading productivity below productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.5: The NGHE Solution shall provide Telemetry (productivity metrics, task completion, operator mode, safety status) to Site Manager for real-time and post-operation productivity verification.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power, fuel-efficient engine with optimized hydraulic system and bucket/blade geometry to maximize material throughput per cycle; pair with on-site operator cabin and tele-operation subsystem; implement real-time safety monitoring and geofence enforcement.

DP-B: Deploy a modular Vehicle platform with swappable work attachments (bucket, blade, grapple) and dual control interfaces (on-site joystick + remote gamepad); integrate Safety Services as a middleware layer that validates all commands before execution; stream Telemetry to a cloud-based analytics dashboard for productivity tracking.

DP-C: Architect the NGHE Solution as a closed-loop system: Vehicle executes tasks autonomously within a defined work zone using GPS/GNSS and inertial guidance; on-site and tele-operators supervise and intervene only when autonomous mode confidence drops below threshold; Safety Services enforces hard constraints (speed, load, stability) independent of operator input.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Deploy a modular Vehicle platform with swappable work attachments and dual control interfaces (on-site joystick + remote gamepad); integrate Safety Services as a middleware layer that validates all commands before execution; stream Telemetry to a cloud-based analytics dashboard for productivity tracking.

potential_sources: Coupling between Vehicle mechanical design and control interface responsiveness: joystick/gamepad latency and force feedback must match operator expectations for safe, productive task execution. Mitigation: standardize control mapping and test with representative operators.

Coupling between Safety Services command validation and operator task execution: overly restrictive safety constraints may prevent productive work; overly permissive constraints may allow unsafe states. Mitigation: parameterize safety thresholds and validate against safety plan requirements.

Coupling between Telemetry streaming frequency and network bandwidth: high-frequency telemetry improves real-time monitoring but increases network load, potentially degrading tele-operation responsiveness. Mitigation: implement adaptive telemetry sampling based on network congestion.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits moderate coupling between control interface design and safety constraint enforcement. The modular architecture (swappable attachments, dual interfaces) reduces coupling compared to DP-A (fixed design) and DP-C (autonomous mode adds complexity and safety criticality). DP-B is preferred because it maintains operator-in-the-loop control, which is essential for high-safety-criticality operations in variable worksite conditions.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: On-site and tele-operators can achieve comparable task execution rates with equivalent training and interface design. Risk: Tele-operation latency and reduced situational awareness may inherently limit productivity; mitigation requires empirical validation during trials.

Assumption: Safety Services middleware can validate commands with <100 ms latency without blocking operator responsiveness. Risk: Validation overhead may introduce perceptible lag; mitigation: implement asynchronous validation with fallback to operator override under safety supervisor approval.

Assumption: Telemetry dashboard provides sufficient real-time visibility for Site Manager to detect productivity degradation and intervene. Risk: Dashboard latency or incomplete data may mask safety or productivity issues; mitigation: implement alerting thresholds and cross-check with on-site safety audit.

Assumption: Network Link (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) maintains sufficient bandwidth and latency for dual-operator modes. Risk: Network congestion or outage may degrade tele-operation or safety alert delivery; mitigation: implement fallback to on-site operator and local safety enforcement.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify: (1) control interface latency budget (e.g., <200 ms round-trip for joystick, <500 ms for gamepad); (2) Safety Services validation logic (e.g., hard constraints: speed ≤ 10 km/h in geofence, load ≤ rated capacity; soft constraints: stability margin ≥ 15%); (3) Telemetry sampling strategy (e.g., 1 Hz for productivity metrics, 10 Hz for safety-critical state, adaptive based on network congestion); (4) fallback behavior on network loss (e.g., Vehicle enters safe hold state, on-site operator takes control).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the overall system architecture, interfaces, and acceptance criteria. Responsibility: ensure that all subsystems work together to achieve productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits Telemetry (productivity metrics: m³/h, ton/h; task completion status; operator mode; safety status; Vehicle health) to Site Manager dashboard and post-operation analytics. Frequency: 1 Hz nominal, adaptive based on network congestion. Format: JSON over MQTT or REST API.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives Tasking commands from Site Manager (task definition: dig, load, transport, dump; work zone boundaries; priority; estimated duration) and from on-site/tele-operators (real-time control: movement, attachment actuation, mode selection). Latency requirement: <500 ms for Site Manager tasks, <200 ms for operator commands.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits SafetyAlert (geofence violation, load limit exceeded, stability margin low, operator override, network loss) to Safety Manager and on-site safety supervisor. Latency requirement: <100 ms for critical alerts. Format: structured alert with severity, timestamp, context.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution depends on Network Link for all remote communication (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). Network Link is external to NGHE Solution and owned by site infrastructure. NGHE Solution must implement fallback behavior on network loss (e.g., Vehicle safe hold, on-site operator takeover).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level boundary for this FR. It owns the overall system architecture, integration of Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services, and acceptance criteria. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link is external (site infrastructure). Containment is high because all functional and design decisions flow through NGHE Solution.

Vehicle owns mechanical capability (engine, hydraulics, attachments) and on-site operator interface, which directly contribute to productivity. However, Vehicle alone cannot achieve productivity_target without Teleoperation (for remote operations) and Safety Services (for constraint enforcement). Diffusion is high because productivity depends on operator effectiveness and safety compliance, which are external to Vehicle.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and command transmission, which is necessary for tele-operator productivity. However, Teleoperation depends on Vehicle mechanical capability and Safety Services validation. Diffusion is high because productivity is a joint outcome of Teleoperation + Vehicle + Safety Services.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (geofence, load limits, stability) and safety monitoring. While Safety Services is critical for safe operations, it does not directly drive productivity; it constrains the operational envelope. Diffusion is high because Safety Services must integrate with Vehicle (for state monitoring) and both operator interfaces (for command validation).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The refined FR (productivity achievement with safety compliance) is best contained within NGHE Solution because: (1) productivity is a system-level outcome that depends on Vehicle capability, operator effectiveness (on-site and remote), and safety constraint enforcement; (2) NGHE Solution owns the integration logic that orchestrates these subsystems; (3) NGHE Solution is responsible for end-to-end acceptance criteria verification (productivity_target measurement, safety plan compliance). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to NGHE Solution's productivity objective but do not independently own it.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution becomes a 'catch-all' integration module without clear ownership of specific design decisions. Mitigation: decompose NGHE Solution into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.5) and assign each to a specific sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with clear interfaces.

Risk: Productivity measurement and verification logic is not clearly assigned. Mitigation: explicitly assign FR-NGHE-001.5 (Telemetry provision) to NGHE Solution and define measurement protocol (sampling frequency, data validation, aggregation method).

Risk: Network Link is external to NGHE Solution but critical for tele-operation and safety alerts. Mitigation: define fallback behavior on network loss (e.g., Vehicle safe hold, on-site operator takeover) and test during trials.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign NGHE Solution as the IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001 (top-level productivity KPI). Decompose into sub-FRs and assign each to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, or NGHE Solution integration layer. Define clear interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and ownership boundaries. Implement explicit fallback logic for network loss and operator mode transitions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (modular Vehicle with dual control interfaces, Safety Services middleware, Telemetry dashboard) directly addresses the refined FR (productivity achievement with safety compliance). DP-B supports both on-site and tele-operator modes, integrates safety constraint enforcement, and provides visibility for productivity measurement. The DP is feasible, testable, and aligns with high-safety-criticality constraints.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Productivity measurement logic is distributed across Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (operator effectiveness), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and NGHE Solution (aggregation). Mitigation: centralize Telemetry collection in NGHE Solution and define clear measurement protocol.

Diffusion Risk 2: Safety constraint enforcement (Safety Services) must integrate with both operator interfaces (on-site joystick, remote gamepad) and Vehicle state monitoring. Mitigation: implement Safety Services as a middleware layer with standardized command validation API.
Diffusion Risk 3: Network Link is external to NGHE Solution but critical for tele-operation and safety alerts. Mitigation: implement fallback behavior on network loss and test during trials.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The DP (modular architecture with dual interfaces and middleware safety enforcement) aligns well with NGHE Solution's role as the orchestration layer. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined and traceable to DP design decisions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 (productivity_target achievement) is feasible via representative operations trials. Measurement protocol: log task execution (dig, load, transport, dump cycles), measure output (m³/h or ton/h), compare against productivity_target. Verification of safety compliance is feasible via incident/near-miss logs and safety audit checklist. Both measurements are traceable to NGHE Solution's Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.5) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution integration layer. Each sub-FR has a clear owner and measurable acceptance criteria. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are defined and traceable to sub-FRs.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Refine DP-B to specify control interface latency budgets (<200 ms for joystick, <500 ms for gamepad) and Safety Services validation logic (hard constraints: speed, load, stability; soft constraints: operator override with safety supervisor approval).

Revision 2: Define Telemetry sampling strategy (1 Hz for productivity metrics, 10 Hz for safety-critical state, adaptive based on network congestion) and measurement protocol (task cycle definition, output calculation, data validation).
Revision 3: Specify fallback behavior on network loss (Vehicle safe hold, on-site operator takeover, SafetyAlert to Safety Manager) and test during trials.
Revision 4: Clarify ownership of productivity measurement and verification logic within NGHE Solution (e.g., dedicated Telemetry aggregation module or function).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that has been successfully refined into a coherent FR with clear DP and IM assignment. The refined FR is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.5) that address Vehicle capability, on-site operator effectiveness, tele-operator effectiveness, Safety Services constraint enforcement, and Telemetry provision. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define detailed design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to allocate design responsibility and define testable acceptance criteria for each sub-FR.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR explicitly acknowledges that productivity is a system-level outcome that depends on multiple subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and operator modes (on-site, remote). The DP (modular Vehicle with dual control interfaces, Safety Services middleware, Telemetry dashboard) is sound but requires further refinement at the next level to specify control latency budgets, safety constraint logic, and Telemetry sampling strategy. The IM assignment (NGHE Solution as top-level boundary) is appropriate but requires decomposition into sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with clear ownership and interfaces. Proceeding to ZIGZAG_DOWN allows each sub-FR to be decomposed independently while maintaining traceability to the top-level productivity objective.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Tele-operator shall be able to command and monitor the Vehicle via Teleoperation with sufficient latency, bandwidth, and feedback fidelity to sustain productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services shall continuously monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, load limits, stability) without degrading productivity below productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.5: The NGHE Solution shall provide Telemetry (productivity metrics, task completion, operator mode, safety status) to Site Manager for real-time and post-operation productivity verification.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Productivity_target is defined by customer and is achievable with conventional heavy equipment technology (fuel-efficient engine, optimized hydraulics, standard attachments).
Assumption 2: On-site and tele-operators can achieve comparable task execution rates with equivalent training and interface design. Empirical validation required during trials.

Assumption 3: Safety Services middleware can validate commands with <100 ms latency without blocking operator responsiveness. Asynchronous validation with fallback to operator override under safety supervisor approval.

Assumption 4: Network Link (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) maintains sufficient bandwidth and latency for dual-operator modes. Fallback to on-site operator and local safety enforcement on network loss.
Assumption 5: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and can be translated into Safety Services constraints (geofence, load limits, stability margins).
Assumption 6: Representative operations trials can be conducted under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level) to verify productivity_target achievement.
open_risks: Risk 1 (High): Tele-operation latency and reduced situational awareness may inherently limit productivity compared to on-site operation. Mitigation: empirical validation during trials with representative operators and task profiles.

Risk 2 (High): Safety Services constraint enforcement may conflict with productivity objectives (e.g., speed limits reduce throughput). Mitigation: parameterize safety thresholds and validate against safety plan requirements; implement operator override with safety supervisor approval.

Risk 3 (Medium): Network Link outage or congestion may degrade tele-operation responsiveness or delay safety alerts. Mitigation: implement fallback behavior (Vehicle safe hold, on-site operator takeover) and test during trials.

Risk 4 (Medium): Productivity measurement protocol may not capture all factors affecting productivity (operator skill, task complexity, environmental conditions). Mitigation: define representative operations conditions and control variables during trials.

Risk 5 (Medium): Modular attachment design (swappable bucket, blade, grapple) may introduce mechanical inefficiencies or interface complexity. Mitigation: validate attachment performance during trials and standardize interface design.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Productivity_target definition from customer (m³/h or ton/h, task profile, soil type, equipment configuration).
Evidence 2: Worksite safety plan and operational policies (speed limits, geofence, load limits, stability margins, operator qualifications).
Evidence 3: Control interface latency measurements (joystick, gamepad, network round-trip) during representative operations.
Evidence 4: Safety Services validation logic specification (hard constraints, soft constraints, operator override conditions).
Evidence 5: Telemetry sampling strategy and measurement protocol (task cycle definition, output calculation, data validation, aggregation method).
Evidence 6: Network Link performance characterization (bandwidth, latency, availability, fallback behavior).
Evidence 7: Representative operations trial results (productivity achieved, safety incidents, operator feedback, environmental conditions).
Evidence 8: Acceptance criteria verification (productivity_target met, safety plan complied, all sub-FRs satisfied).
